title: Constitutional Amendments
description: All 27 amendments to the U.S. Constitution

cards.back: Freedom of religion, speech, press, assembly, and petition
Right to keep and bear arms
No quartering of soldiers in private homes without consent
Protection against unreasonable searches and seizures
Right to due process, protection against double jeopardy and self-incrimination
Right to a speedy and public trial, impartial jury, and legal counsel
Right to jury trial in civil cases exceeding $20
Protection against excessive bail, fines, and cruel and unusual punishment
Rights not listed in the Constitution are retained by the people
Powers not delegated to the federal government are reserved to the states or the people
States cannot be sued in federal court by citizens of another state or foreign country
Separate Electoral College votes for President and Vice President
Abolition of slavery
Citizenship, due process, and equal protection under the law
Right to vote cannot be denied based on race, color, or previous servitude
Congress can levy an income tax
Direct election of U.S. Senators by popular vote
Prohibition of alcohol (later repealed by 21st Amendment)
Women's right to vote
Changed inauguration date to January 20; lame duck amendment
Repeal of Prohibition (18th Amendment)
Presidents limited to two terms in office
Washington D.C. granted Electoral College votes
Abolition of poll taxes in federal elections
Presidential succession and disability procedures
Voting age lowered to 18
Congressional pay changes take effect after the next election
